data.title: European Gas Prices in Tight Range, With Focus on Weather Outlook—Market Talk — Market Talk
data.description: European natural-gas prices are lower in early trading, with the benchmark Dutch TTF contract down 2.3% to 30.45 euros a megawatt hour. Prices have been broadly rangebound in recent weeks, hovering between 30 and 32 euros a megawatt hour. "Recent temperatures have dropped, boosting heating demand," ANZ analysts say. "However, the latest weather models are signalling a slower return to milder conditions next month." According to industry group Gas Infrastructure Europe, EU gas storage is currently 80.7% full.(giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

data.title: Lithuania Industrial Output Growth Accelerates in October
data.description:
Lithuania’s industrial production increased by 1.8% year-on-year in October 2025, accelerating from a downwardly revised 0.2% rise in the previous month.

The upturn was mainly driven by a rebound in manufacturing (1.4% vs. -0.1% in September) and by a stronger output growth in electricity, gas, steam, and air conditioning supply (9.4% vs. 5.3%).

In contrast, activity growth in mining and quarrying moderated (2.6% vs. 3.9%), while production in water supply, sewerage, waste management, and remediation services contracted further (-11.6% vs. -8.1%).

On a monthly basis, industrial output declined 0.5%, following a downwardly revised 2.0% drop in September.

data.title: Correction: INITIATOR PHARMA: Q3 2025 REPORT
data.description:
By mistake a draft version of the press release was published at 08:30 today, where the business highlights and the relevant period had not been updated. This has now been corrected.

Business highlights in Q3 2025

  • In July the Company announced the outcome of the Company’s rights issue of up to 14,039,590 shares (the “Rights Issue”), for which the subscription period ended on 26 June 2025. In the Rights Issue, 6,133,159 shares, corresponding to around 43.7 percent of the Rights Issue were subscribed through subscription rights. Additionally, 277,458 shares, corresponding to around 2.0 percent of the Rights Issue were subscribed without subscription rights. 5,670,164 shares, corresponding to around 40.4 percent of the Rights Issue were subscribed through guarantee undertakings. The Rights Issue will provide the Company with proceeds of approximately SEK 48.3 million before deduction of costs related to the Rights Issue.
  • In September the Company announced the submission of a clinical trial application (CTA) to the UK’s Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA), and local ethics committee in the UK for the approval to conduct a Phase IIa clinical proof-of-concept study in women suffering from vulvodynia, a severe chronic pain condition affecting approximately 10 percent of women worldwide. The study will be conducted together with MAC Clinical Research in the UK.

Business highlights after this reporting period

  • In November the Company announced that its Clinical Trial Application (CTA) for the planned Phase IIa clinical proof-of-concept study in women with vulvodynia has been approved by the UK Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) and a local ethics committee. First patients are expected to be enrolled in Q4 2025.

Financial Highlights

  • Third Quarter (2025-07-01 – 2025-09-30)
  • Net revenue were KDKK 0 (0)
  • Operating loss, EBIT was KDKK -4,318 (-3,233)
  • Earnings per share before and after dilution was DKK -0.06 (-0.06)
  • Cash: KDKK 28,674 (11,979)
  • Solidity: 88% (92%)
  • First Nine Months (2025-01-01 – 2025-09-30)
  • Net revenues were TDKK 0 (0)
  • Operating loss, EBIT was TDKK -10,815 (-11,801)
  • Earnings per share before and after dilution was DKK –0.16 (-0.22)

Solidity: equity divided by assets.

The Board of Directors have decided that interim reports will be published in English only.

Link to the full report

https://www.initiatorpharma.com/en/investors/reports-and-presentations/

data.title: French Services PMI Expands for First Time Since 2024
data.description:
The HCOB France Services PMI rose to 50.8 in October 2025, recovering from 48.0 in the previous month and surpassing market expectations of 48.3, flash estimates showed.

The latest reading marked the sector’s first return to expansion since August 2024, supported by a marginal increase in output amid stronger sales performance and a modest pickup in demand, as indicated by new business inflows.

Moreover, job losses in the services sector were less pronounced than in manufacturing, suggesting that firms are making efforts to preserve staffing levels amid gradually stabilizing activity.

On the price front, service providers continued to face rising input costs, driven primarily by higher wages and increased materials expenses.

However, output prices remained unchanged, highlighting ongoing constraints on pricing power across the sector.

data.title: DAX Drops on Friday
data.description:
Germany's DAX traded more than 1% lower near the 23,000 mark on Friday, pulling back from yesterday's advance, in line with other European peers.

Concerns over AI valuations have resurfaced, even amid Nvidia’s strong earnings, driving volatility in markets.

Meanwhile, speculation about a potential peace deal in Ukraine has also been influencing investor sentiment in recent days.

Meanwhile, traders assessed a batch of PMI data from several economies.

In Germany, flash PMI data indicated continued weakness in manufacturing, alongside a slowdown in services growth.

data.title: U.S. Futures Edge Up, European Stocks Decline
data.description:
U.S. futures for the S&P 500 were up 0.2% and futures for the Dow Jones Industrial Average climbed 0.3%. Changes in futures do not necessarily predict movements after the opening bell.

In Europe, the Stoxx Europe 600 declined 1% in morning trading. CTS Eventim rose 6.7% and Euronext rose 1.6%. On the other hand, Renk Group slipped 7.8%, and Siemens Energy dropped 7.3%. The FTSE 100 lost 0.8%. Other stocks in Europe were down as France's CAC 40 decreased 0.8% and Germany's DAX fell 1.2%.

The Wall Street Journal Dollar Index dropped 0.2% to 97.45.

In commodities, Brent crude fell 1.5% to $62.40 a barrel, and WTI crude fell 1.8% to $57.91 a barrel. The European benchmark price for natural gas, the Dutch futures contract TTF, was down 2.5% to 30.38 euros a megawatt hour.

The German 10-year Bund yield fell by 2 basis points to 2.7% from 2.725%, and the yield on the 10-year U.S. Treasury declined by 1 basis point to 4.081% from 4.088%. Bond prices and yields move in opposite directions.

Stocks in Asia were down as Japan's Nikkei 225 index fell 2.4%, and Hong Kong's Hang Seng declined 2.4%. China's benchmark Shanghai Composite declined 2.4%.

-This article was automatically generated by MarketWatch.

Write to Barcelona Editors at barcelonaeditors@dowjones.com

data.title: Malaysia's 2026 Inflation Expected to Edge Higher, Supported by Firmer Demand — Market Talk
data.description: Malaysia's inflation is expected to edge up to 1.8% in 2026, compared with an expected 1.5% this year, supported by firmer domestic demand, resilient consumption and expansionary fiscal measures, RHB senior economist Chin Yee Sian says in a note. However, the increase may remain manageable--below the long-term average of 2%--as orderly policy implementation and softer global commodity prices help contain cost pressures, she says. Demand-led pressures may rise with stronger disposable incomes, while policy changes such as carbon tax and sin-tax adjustments should have mild effects. Transport inflation is likely to stay soft due to lower RON95 fuel prices, while the new automatic fuel adjustment for electricity tariffs may cause some monthly variability, she adds. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: Germany Services Activity Growth Slows
data.description:
The HCOB Germany Services PMI fell to 52.7 in November 2025 from a 29-month high of 54.6 in October, preliminary data showed.

Despite the decline, the reading remained solid overall and was in line with the long-run average.

New business in the services sector continued to grow, supported by higher demand and increased customer enquiries, but the rate of expansion softened.

Hiring continues, albeit more cautiously.

Meanwhile, backlogs of work returned to contraction after rising for the first time in 18 months in the previous month.

On the price front, services firms continued to report rising operating expenses in November, which they attributed in large part to wage pressures.

On the other hand, the rise in prices charged slowed.

Lastly, service providers remained optimistic about activity over the next year, though the degree of confidence fell to its weakest level since June.

data.title: Euro zone bond yields fall from 6-week high
data.description:
Key points:
  • Eyes on stocks sell off, Fed policy outlook
  • ECB likely to maintain current interest rates for now
  • U.S. jobs data shows employment growth slowdown, not collapse

By Samuel Indyk

Germany's 10-year bond yield retreated from a six-week high on Friday, with investors remaining focused on the sell-off in global equities and expectations for Federal Reserve policy.

Among euro-denominated assets, investors often turn to German bonds when global risk sentiment sours, given the country's very low default risk and high bond market liquidity.

Bond yields move inversely with prices.

Stocks in Europe opened with steep losses, although U.S. futures were recovering slightly after a sharp reversal on Thursday that saw the S&P 500 close lower by 1.5%.

Germany's 10-year bond yield (DE10YT=RR), the benchmark for the euro zone, was down 2.5 basis points (bps) at 2.695%, having hit its highest in six weeks on Thursday at 2.741%.

On Friday, investors were watching the euro zone PMIs for any indication whether business activity in the bloc is improving at the end of the year, after a strong increase last month.

"Consensus is looking for marginal changes only," said Michiel Tukker, ING senior rates strategist.

"As such, this would support a picture of slow but still resilient growth."

EYES ON STOCKS, FED

With the European Central Bank looking likely to hold interest rates for the foreseeable future, investors in euro zone bonds were turning their attention elsewhere, including the outlook for Fed policy.

Fed officials have generally signalled they are comfortable with keeping interest rates on hold at the December meeting, given still sticky inflation and a dearth of data due to the government shutdown.

September's delayed U.S. jobs data, released on Thursday, would likely have done little to swing policymakers either way, as job growth accelerated but the unemployment rate ticked up.

"As a whole, while the report is largely history already, in the absence of newer data, it continues to point to only a slowdown in employment growth, not a collapse, and is not screaming for more Fed rate cuts either," said Jan von Gerich, chief analyst at Nordea.

Markets are now pricing about a 25% chance of a cut next month, down from about 90% just a couple of weeks ago.

Shorter-end yields, which are more sensitive to changes in interest rate policy, were also falling slightly on Friday.

Germany's two-year yield (DE2YT=RR) was down 2 bps at 2.004%.

data.title: Mobvista: Revenue up 27.6% YoY, adjusted net profit up 126%, but net loss due to derivative losses
data.description:
Revenue grew 27.6% year-over-year to $531.6 million, led by Mintegral's strong performance and AI-driven products. Adjusted net profit surged 126% to $24.1 million, but a large non-cash loss from convertible bond derivatives resulted in a net loss for the period.

Original document: Mobvista Incorporation [1860] Earnings Release — Nov. 21 2025

Disclaimer
This is an AI-generated summary and may contain inaccuracies. Please verify any important information with the original source.

data.title: GEK Terna's nine-month profit rises on concessions boost
data.description:
Greek infrastructure and energy group GEK Terna reported a 66.2% jump in adjusted core profit for the first nine months of 2025, driven by a surge in concession revenues.

The group's adjusted earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortisation (EBITDA) for the January-to-September period rose to 463.9 million euros ($535.34 million), compared to 279.2 million in the same period last year.

The concessions segment contributed 57% of group adjusted core profit and its adjusted EBITDA more than doubled to 267.3 million euros, supported by higher motorway traffic and the addition of Attiki Odos, which contributed 133.7 million euros.

The construction segment saw its adjusted EBITDA rise 35.7% to 123.4 million euros, as the company's signed backlog expanded to 6.8 billion euros at the end of September, up from 4.1 billion at the close of 2024, with the total backlog reaching 9.2 billion euros.

The Athens-listed group said it expects profitability gains to remain sustainable, supported by long-term concession projects.

Upcoming milestones include the signing of the Egnatia Odos concession by the end of 2025, the anticipated signing of the North Crete motorway concession in early 2026, and progress on its Kastelli Airport and Hellinikon resort projects, it added.

($1 = 0.8666 euros)

data.title: Oil Extends Decline on Easing Geopolitical Concerns — Market Talk
data.description: Oil prices extend losses in early trading after Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky agreed to work on a U.S.-drafted peace plan, easing geopolitical concerns. Brent crude is down 1.2% at $62.62 a barrel, while WTI is down 1.4% at $58.15 a barrel. Both benchmarks are headed for a weekly loss of around 3% as prospects of an oversupplied market continue to weigh on sentiment. Meanwhile, market watchers continue to closely monitor Russian flows as U.S. sanctions on Russia's Rosneft and Lukoil come into effect. "Any peace deal could have broader implications for the oil market," analysts at ANZ say. "If accompanied by the elimination of U.S. sanctions on Russian oil, it could release more oil onto the global market." (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

data.title: European Oil Stocks Slide as Geopolitical Premium Eases — Market Talk
data.description: Oil stocks fall in opening trade as prices weaken on easing geopolitical tensions. Brent crude is down 1.5% to $62.45 a barrel and WTI is down 1.8% to $57.96 a barrel. The fall comes as peace plan between Ukraine and Russia appears to progress. Meanwhile, traders are skeptical that the latest sanctions on Russia's Rosneft and Lukoil will be effective, given that Lukoil's share of oil from an Iraqi field continues to enter the market, ANZ analysts write. In London, Shell and BP fall by more than 1% while Harbour Energy drops around 4%. France's TotalEnergies slides 1.8%. Spain's Repsol falls 3.5% and Italy's Eni drops 1.5%. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

data.title: Enad Global 7's Largest Shareholder Sends Letter Ahead Of EGM
data.description:
Enad Global 7 AB (publ) :
